text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: Bad teeth can be painful and worse. They can even be deadly.
zh: 牙齿不好可能会带来痛苦，甚至更严重的后果。它们甚至可能致命。

text: Infections of the gums and teeth can release bacteria into the blood system.
zh: 牙龈和牙齿感染会将细菌释放进血液系统。

text: Those bacteria can increase the chances of a heart attack or stroke
zh: 这些细菌会增加心脏病发作或中风的几率，

text: and worsen the effects of other diseases.
zh: 并使其他疾病的病情恶化。

text: And adults are not the only ones at risk.
zh: 而成年人并不是唯一有风险的人群。

text: For example, in 2007, doctors in the Washington area
zh: 例如，在2007年，华盛顿地区的医生

text: said a boy died when a tooth infection spread to his brain.
zh: 称一名男孩因牙齿感染扩散到大脑而死亡。

text: They said it might have been prevented had he received the dental care he needed.
zh: 他们说，如果他及时接受所需的牙科护理，本可以避免这种情况。

text: He was 12 years old. Experts at the National Institutes of Health
zh: 他当时只有12岁。美国国立卫生研究院的专家

text: say good dental care starts at birth.
zh: 表示，良好的牙齿护理应从出生就开始。

text: Breast milk, they say, is the best food for the healthy development of teeth.
zh: 他们说，母乳是促进牙齿健康发育的最佳食物。

text: Breast milk can help slow bacterial growth and acid production in the mouth.
zh: 母乳有助于减缓口腔中细菌的生长和酸的产生。

text: But dentists say a baby's gums and early teeth should be cleaned after each feeding.
zh: 但牙医建议，每次喂奶后都应清洁婴儿的牙龈和早期牙齿。

text: Use a cloth with a little warm water. Do the same if a baby is fed with a bottle.
zh: 可用蘸有少量温水的布来清洁。如果用奶瓶喂养，也应同样处理。

text: Experts say if you decide to put your baby to sleep with a bottle, only give the child water.
zh: 专家表示，如果你打算让宝宝叼着奶瓶入睡，瓶里只能装水。

text: When baby teeth begin to appear, here, you can clean them with a wet toothbrush.
zh: 当乳牙开始萌出时，可以用湿润的牙刷清洁。

text: Dentists say it is important to find soft toothbrushes made especially for babies
zh: 牙医说，重要的是要选用专为婴儿设计的软毛牙刷，

text: and to use them very gently.
zh: 并且刷牙时动作要非常轻柔。

text: The use of fluoride to protect teeth is common in many parts of the world.
zh: 使用氟化物保护牙齿在世界许多地方都很普遍。

text: For example, it is often added to drinking water supplies.
zh: 例如，氟化物常被添加到饮用水供应中。

text: The fluoride mixes with enamel, the hard surface on teeth,
zh: 氟会与牙齿表面坚硬的珐琅质结合，

text: to help prevent holes or cavities from forming.
zh: 有助于防止蛀牙或龋洞的形成。

text: But young children often swallow toothpaste when they brush.
zh: 但幼儿刷牙时常常会咽下牙膏。

text: The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry
zh: 美国儿童牙科学会

text: notes that swallowing fluoridated toothpaste can cause problems.
zh: 指出，吞咽含氟牙膏可能会带来问题。

text: So young children should be carefully supervised when they brush their teeth.
zh: 因此，幼儿刷牙时应有大人在旁仔细看护。

text: And only a small amount of fluoridated toothpaste the size of a pea should be used.
zh: 而且每次使用的含氟牙膏量应只有豌豆大小。

text: Parents often wonder what effect thumb sucking or sucking
zh: 家长常常想知道，吸吮拇指或

text: on a pacifier might have on their baby's teeth.
zh: 使用安抚奶嘴会对宝宝的牙齿产生什么影响。

text: Dental experts generally agree that this is fine early in life.
zh: 牙科专家普遍认为，在幼年时期这样做是没问题的。

text: The American Academy of Family Physicians says most children stop sucking their thumb by the age of four.
zh: 美国家庭医师学会表示，大多数孩子在四岁前会停止吸吮拇指。

text: If it continues, the group advises parents to talk to their child's dentist or doctor.
zh: 如果这种习惯持续下去，该学会建议家长咨询孩子的牙医或医生。

text: It could interfere with with the correct development of permanent teeth.
zh: 因为这可能会影响恒牙的正常发育。

text: Dentists say children should have their first dental visit
zh: 牙医建议，孩子第一次看牙医

text: at least by the time they are one year old.
zh: 最迟应在一岁之前。

text: They say babies should be examined when their first teeth appear,
zh: 他们说，婴儿的第一颗牙齿萌出时就应接受检查，

text: usually at around six months.
zh: 通常大约在六个月大的时候。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villareal.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是亚历克斯·比利亚雷亚尔。

text: You can learn English and much more at voaspecialenglish .com
zh: 您可以登录voaspecialenglish.com学习英语及更多内容，

text: and the VOA Learning English page on Facebook.
zh: 也可以关注Facebook上的“美国之音学英语”页面。
